source.title: Large Visual Stimuli Induce Two Distinct Gamma Oscillations in Primate Visual Cortex

confidence_rationale: Primate LFP and human EEG data showing two functionally distinct gamma bands (slow 25-45 Hz and fast 45-70 Hz) with different spatial coherence profiles in V1. The dual-gamma finding partially dissociates local feature processing from long-range integration relevant to entity binding. Genuine cross-species replication (monkey + human) strengthens transfer to REE, though the mechanism linking dual gammas to entity emergence remains a theoretical step.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Large visual stimuli induce two distinct gamma oscillations in primate V1 (bonnet monkeys) and human EEG: slow gamma (25-45 Hz) reflecting long-range processing and fast gamma (45-70 Hz) reflecting short-range processing, with distinct tuning for orientation, contrast, drift speed, and size.
mapping.ree_translation: Entity emergence in REE requires binding local features (handled by fast, short-range processes) with more global context (handled by slow, long-range processes). The dual-gamma dissociation provides a mechanistic scaffold: fast gamma could support local feature detection while slow gamma could mediate the inter-region synchrony needed to bind features into a unified entity representation in z_world.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Both gamma rhythms are measured in primary visual cortex in response to simple grating stimuli. Entity formation at the level REE requires -- multi-attribute objects with causal histories and goal/harm relevance -- likely requires higher cortical areas (IT, PFC) beyond V1. The mapping from V1 dual-gamma to REE entity binding is an extrapolation. Additionally, human EEG cannot resolve laminar or single-unit dynamics, limiting mechanistic inference.
mapping.source_context: Awake bonnet monkeys (LFP recordings in V1) and human subjects (EEG), large grating stimuli of varying orientation, contrast, size, and drift speed.

confidence_components.notes: Strong cross-species replication (primate LFP + human EEG) raises source quality. Mapping fidelity is limited by the V1/grating context; the jump to abstract entity binding requires invoking additional mechanisms not tested here. Transfer risk moderate -- gamma oscillations in visual cortex are well-established but REE entity emergence is a more abstract cognitive operation.
